reasoning_content: We need to check if 193 is a prime number. Quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. 193 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 1+9+3=13, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Ends with 3, not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*27=189, remainder 4, so not divisible. 11: 11*17=187, remainder 6; 11*18=198 too high, so no. 13: 13*14=182, remainder 11; 13*15=195, too high, so no. 17: 17*11=187, remainder 6; 17*12=204 too high. 19: 19*10=190, remainder 3; 19*11=209 too high. 23: 23*8=184, remainder 9; 23*9=207 too high. Next prime is 29: 29*6=174, remainder 19; 29*7=203 too high. 31: 31*6=186, remainder 7; 31*7=217 too high. 37: 37*5=185, remainder 8; 37*6=222 too high. 41: 41*4=164, remainder 29; 41*5=205 too high. 43: 43*4=172, remainder 21; 43*5=215 too high. 47: 47*4=188, remainder 5; 47*5=235 too high. Now, we only need to check up to sqrt(193). sqrt(193) is about 13.89, because 14^2=196, so we only need to check primes up to 13. Actually sqrt(193) < 14, so we only need to check primes up to 13. We've checked 2,3,5,7,11,13. None divide. So 193 is prime. Quick sanity: 193 is indeed a prime number. Answer: prime.
